creator: roam@orbitel.bg
text:
On Wed, Mar 07, 2001 at 09:23:53AM +0100, Anton Berezin wrote:
> On Wed, Mar 07, 2001 at 10:16:54AM +0200, Peter Pentchev wrote:
> > On Tue, Mar 06, 2001 at 09:47:00PM +0100, Anton Berezin wrote:
> > > New version:
> > > 
> > > - libesmtp is now at 0.5;
> > > - incorporate roam's suggestions to improve the port;
> > > - add a little bit of docs.
> > 
> > Just a minor comment..
> > 
> > > sed 's/^X//' >libesmtp/pkg-comment << 'END-of-libesmtp/pkg-comment'
> > > XlibESMTP - A Library for Posting Electronic Mail
> > > END-of-libesmtp/pkg-comment
> 
> > portlint complains that this should begin with a capital; is it really
> > necessary to have the libESMTP in front?  How about just: A library
> > for posting electronic mail
> 
> Umm.  That's fine with me.

The pkg-comment is mainly used in building the package index and the README
file; in both those cases, there is 'libesmtp' in the immediate vicinity
of the pkg-comment text.

This is just a minor style matter, and if you desire, I could leave
the libESMTP in front; it's your call :)

G'luck,
Peter

-- 
Do you think anybody has ever had *precisely this thought* before?
